title: Child Workflow: Compliance - Remediate Device
description: Optional. Applies a corrective config to a single non-compliant device. Requires human approval before any change. Backs up device first. Re-evaluates after to confirm fix. Off by default.

device_name.description: Device to remediate. Must exist in ConfigurationManager.

corrective_config.description: IOS/NX-OS configuration commands to push. One command per line.

rules.description: Rules to re-evaluate after fix. Usually the subset that failed on this device.

remediation_status.description: pass = fix applied and post-eval passed, fail = fix applied but post-eval failed, rejected = operator rejected at approval gate

backup_id.description: ConfigurationManager backup reference for the pre-remediation config snapshot

post_eval_results.description: Per-rule results after fix applied. Empty if remediation was rejected or backup failed.

internal_steps: 2. ViewData manual task — show operator: device_name, violations, corrective_config
4. Call compliance_evaluate.py (IAG5) with fresh config + rules
5. Set remediation_status = pass if all previously-failing rules now pass, else fail
safety_rules: Backup is always taken BEFORE any config push — non-negotiable
If backup fails, abort immediately — do not push config
If post-eval fails, flag device but do NOT retry automatically
remediation_enabled must be true in orchestrator inputs to invoke this child
